Title: Genome-Wide Identification of Cotton (Gossypium spp.) Glycerol-3-Phosphate Dehydrogenase (GPDH) Family Members and the Role of GhGPDH5 in Response to Drought Stress

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: GhGPDH5 belongs to group IV of the cotton GPDH family, which corresponds to FAD-dependent GPDHs; it is located on subgenome chromosome A06 and was selected for functional validation because other GPDH genes showed lower expression under drought stress. A predicted protein–protein interaction network suggests GhGPDH5 may interact with other GPDH family members and additional proteins, although these interactions were inferred from Arabidopsis homologs and not experimentally confirmed in cotton.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. GhGPDH5 is a member of the cotton GPDH family located on chromosome A06 and classified as an FAD-dependent GPDH with DAO_C and DAO domains.
2. Transcriptome analyses show that GhGPDH5 is strongly induced by drought, heat, and salt stresses and is highly expressed during early and mid fiber and ovule development.
3. TRV-based VIGS of GhGPDH5 in TM-1 seedlings leads to significantly reduced drought tolerance, with silenced plants exhibiting more severe wilting than controls.
4. GhGPDH5-silenced plants show increased leaf cell death and ROS-associated lesions and fail to close stomata tightly under drought stress, indicating that GhGPDH5 positively regulates drought stress tolerance and stomatal closure in G. hirsutum.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression datasets for G. hirsutum GPDH genes across multiple tissues, fiber and ovule developmental stages, and under cold, heat, drought, and salt stress, downloaded from CottonFGD and visualized as log2(FPKM+1) heatmaps.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing GhGPDH5 significantly reduces drought tolerance in upland cotton seedlings, causing more severe wilting, increased cell death and ROS accumulation in leaves, and impaired stomatal closure under drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Silencing GhGPDH5 leads to increased ROS accumulation and lesion formation in leaves under drought, as shown by intensified DAB staining, indicating a role in maintaining redox homeostasis during drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Acts in the drought stress response pathway to enhance drought tolerance, likely by contributing to osmotic adjustment and ROS/redox regulation and promoting stomatal closure under water deficit.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glycerol-3-phosphate and glycerol metabolism linked to glycolysis, providing precursor for glycerol and triacylglycerol synthesis and contributing to cellular redox and energy metabolism as a glycerol-3-phosphate dehydrogenase.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FAD-dependent glycerol-3-phosphate dehydrogenase (group IV GPDH) containing DAO_C (PF16901) and DAO (PF01266) domains.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: In G. hirsutum, GhGPDH5 expression is strongly induced by drought, heat, and salt stresses, generally increasing with treatment time and then decreasing after peaking around 12 h; it shows high transcript levels during fiber development from 3–20 DPA and elevated expression in ovules from 3–20 DPA.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Abiotic stress treatments including cold, heat, drought, and salt (for transcriptome profiling), and natural drought stress imposed on VIGS plants by withholding water before and after TRV:GhGPDH5 inoculation.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Drought stress tolerance and stomatal movement in cotton; functional characterization of the GPDH gene family member GhGPDH5.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought tolerance; osmotic stress response) and stomatal movement.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Under drought stress, GhGPDH5-silenced plants show increased cell necrosis (stronger trypan blue staining) and more DAB-stained lesions in leaves compared with controls, and their stomatal apertures remain more open rather than tightly closed.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Gossypium hirsutum cv. TM-1 seedlings; TRV:GhGPDH5 recombinant vector; TRV:CLA1 positive control; TRV:00 (pYL192:pYL156:00) negative control; Agrobacterium tumefaciens strain GV3101.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification of GPDH genes using homology search and HMM; RNA-seq-based expression profiling across tissues, fiber and ovule developmental stages, and under cold, heat, drought, and salt stresses; promoter cis-element analysis; miRNA target prediction; protein–protein interaction network prediction; TRV-based VIGS of GhGPDH5 in TM-1; natural drought by withholding water; trypan blue and DAB staining of leaves; stomatal aperture observation by light microscopy; and qPCR to quantify GhGPDH5 expression.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Virus-induced gene silencing (TRV-based VIGS) of GhGPDH5 in TM-1 seedlings, followed by natural drought treatment, trypan blue and DAB leaf staining, stomatal aperture microscopy, and qPCR confirmation of GhGPDH5 knockdown.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a member of the cotton GPDH family through genome-wide BLAST and HMM searches using Arabidopsis GPDH proteins, then prioritized for functional analysis based on relatively high transcript levels under drought stress inferred from RNA-seq heatmaps, and functionally tested by TRV-based VIGS.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: GhGPDH5 is a positive regulator of drought tolerance in upland cotton seedlings; enhancing its activity or selecting favorable alleles could improve drought resistance by promoting stomatal closure, limiting water loss, and maintaining ROS/redox balance, thereby contributing to yield stability under water-limited conditions.
